nodes.context: American criminal family based in Fort Worth, Texas, involved in narcotics trafficking from 1921 to 1947.
Texas-based criminal syndicate arrested in 1936 by the Federal Bureau of Narcotics.
Contemporary Mexican drug trafficker, possible supplier of heroin to the Beland family.

edges.evidence_quote: After the Federal Bureau of Narcotics under Harry J. Anslinger arrested the Texas-based Ginsberg-Kayne-Gordon criminal syndicate in 1936, the Belands were able to take over international trafficking.
edges.context: The Beland family took over international trafficking after the Ginsberg-Kayne-Gordon syndicate was arrested.

edges.evidence_quote: It is not known if the Beland family's supply of heroin came from Lola la Chata, a contemporary Mexican drug trafficker.
edges.context: The text speculates that Lola la Chata may have supplied heroin to the Beland family.
